one_time_description: Land Treaty Trust annual disbursement from $150M Government of Canada land treaty settlement; member distributions and trust fund expenses of $10,756,094 also recorded
economic_notes: First Nation holds $161.7M in restricted trust fund investments from land treaty settlement. GBEs include MCBLP (economic development), WDLP (community economic development), NSILP (forestry and energy), Island View Camp, OLP (forestry), MHL (resource and mining investments). Mahihkan Holdings Ltd incorporated May 2020 to invest in resource and mining entities.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021 (year ended March 31, 2021), not 2024. The $150M land treaty settlement was received in 2020; in 2021 a trust fund disbursement of $3,868,568 was recognized as revenue offset by $11,385,354 in member distributions and trust expenses, resulting in a net deficit of $5,864,173. Long-term debt of $80.2M is a CIBC revolving credit facility secured by trust fund investments, used for economic development. One subsidiary (Missanabie Cree Development Corporation) is fully consolidated; seven entities are accounted for on modified equity basis. No schedule of remuneration was included in the provided text. OSR calculated as total revenue $5,962,090 minus government transfers ($768,469 ISC + $976,282 Mushkegowuk + $348,972 Gov Canada + $29,344 Mocreebec + $126,822 NAN + $117,044 Ontario + $615,424 OFNLP = $2,982,357) = $2,979,733. Prior year total expenses from segmented schedule appear as $4,387,470 but statement of operations shows $3,015,119 excluding land treaty items.
